title: Phenomenon 1-C
wikitext:
{{VoidedTheme|mono}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Phenomenon 1-C: Mass Noclippage}}
[[File:Warning-triangle.png|link=]]
WARNING!
THE M.E.G. HAVE ISSUED A WARNING RELATED TO PHENOMENON 1-C "MASS NOCLIPPAGE". ATTEMPTING TO ENTER THE [[Backrooms Wiki|BACKROOMS]] THROUGH THE PHENOMENON HAS PROVED FATAL. WANDERERS ATTEMPTING TO DO SO HAVE OFTEN BEEN FOUND DISMEMBERED. ATTEMPT TO AVOID ALL INSTANCES OF PHENOMENON 1-C "MASS NOCLIPPAGE".


{{Phenomenon Class|frequency=1|danger=4|effectiveness=4}} ==Description== Phenomenon 1-C, informally referred to as “Mass Noclippage” or “Blips”, are large-scale variations of [[Phenomenon 1]], each manifestation varying in location & scale; ranging from 5 meters up to a mile in diameterManifestations often occur in a circular shape.. The phenomenon has been found to work in tandem with [[Phenomenon 99]]; blips most often lead to Temporary Manifestations of levels. Each individual manifestation of the phenomenon typically lasts only 3-5 seconds, and smaller manifestations often last longer than larger ones. It is theorised by physicist James PollerA high-ranking researcher within the [[M.E.G.]]. that each manifestation radiates radiation, similarly to that of Hawking radiation from black holes. This radiation takes a small piece of energy from the manifestation with it, causing it to slowly decrease in size. The emitted radiation is ionising, believed to be that of gamma. James Poller is also responsible for the Poller Scale: a system devised to determine the strength and danger of these manifestations. The scale ranges from P0 to P9. Manifestations can seemingly also generate [[Phenomenon 99|Temporary Spaces]], similarly to that of [[Object 22]]. These spaces likely form from large pieces of mattere.g. the Collosus blip. forming into levels within the void. Due to the varying lifetimes of each manifestation, wanderers are advised to take caution while investigating the phenomenon. If the blip closes while one is entering, there is a risk of dismemberment and limb loss. Manifestations can occur in both [[The Frontrooms]] and [[Backrooms Wiki|The Backrooms]], though uncommon. These were responsible for the disappearance of the Palast der Republik in Moscow. The phenomenon may also be responsible for other disappearing areas, most notably Atlantis & the city of Vineta.
{{Blurimage|file=Phen1CMassNoclip.jpg|caption=An affected building, located on an unknown level.}}
==History== A few notable manifestations of the phenomenon have occurred, affecting both the Frontrooms & Backrooms respectively. On the 7th of July, 1982, gravitational anomalies began affecting an outpost located on [[Level 4]], and multiple noclippages occurred. This would be referred to as blip "Sanchez", and would result in the death of researcher Kane Korell, referred to as "K.K". K.K. was the first to notice the unusual activity surrounding the outpost, reporting it to the other members, inadvertently saving their lives. The blip spanned ~20 meters, being the first blip to be formally documented. James Poller, a researcher from the outpost, created an official classification scale referred to as the Poller Scale. This has also been used to document & categorise other phenomena. K.K. would be commemorated with a funeral and memorial outside the Poller Research group's main outpost. On the 22nd of February, 2011, numerous unusual anomalies would be detected within [[The Void]]. These anomalies were often detected within the now-destroyed levelInformation on this level is sparse, with very little information known. It is believed this may have been the first iteration of Level 23., due to its instability. A P5 blip occurred, taking large parts of the area alongside it. There were no fatalities. The blip, referred to as blip "Citric", scaled up to 73 meters in diameter, swallowing large pieces of the outpost. The largest documented blip occurred on the 31st of January, 2016. It was determined to be of level P7 on the Poller Scale. There were hundreds of fatalities, occurring within [[Level 339]]. Approximately 1600 meters in diameter, the blip (referred to as Collosus) lasted for just .3 seconds, occurring right before [[Phenomenon 12|the Flicker]]. Collosus has been the only blip determined to be above P5, likely due to the anomalous behaviour surrounding the Flicker. According to the beliefs of some conspiracy theorists, the phenomenon appears to relate to the 1943 USS Eldridge Philadelphia Experiment. The vessel mysteriously disappeared within the Philadelphia Naval Shipyard in Pennsylvania. Theorists believe the phenomenon is the cause of this, with the ship falling into a blip and entering the Backrooms entirely. If this theory is true, it will be the oldest known evidence of a blip. Some other notable manifestations of the phenomenon include: * [[Level 8]] - Instance 1 - 11 meters in diameter, P1 on the Poller Scale; * [[Level 10]] - Instance 4 - 5 meters in diameter, smallest determined blip, P0 on the Poller Scale; * [[Level 11]] - Instance 6 - 21 meters in diameter, previously crowned the largest, P3 on the Poller Scale; * [[Level 79]] - Instance 9 - 15 meters in diameter, P2 on the Poller Scale.
